impact_on_program.next: Prove M_pad<=1 at deployed w; inject free-1 CS pairs into e*p labels
impact_on_program.not_circular: does not use N_ord <= 16*N as the payment bound
impact_on_program.path: M_pad<=1 + CS side-pair mark into t*p (=e*p deployed) => A_SP<=t*p

OPEN.name: OPEN_M_pad_and_side_pair_injection
OPEN.note: Deployed coincidence: t = e = w+1 = 67472, so t·p = e·p. A mark using one side exponent index in {0..e−1} and one field element is the right size; none proved injective yet.
OPEN.statement:
(1) Prove M_pad(z) ≤ 1 for deployed (or for residual after further deletions), or bound M_pad by a small constant.
(2) Inject ordered free-1 CS e-set pairs (U,V) into a set of size ≤ t·p (deployed t=e=w+1, so e·p labels — natural scale).
Together ⇒ A_SP ≤ t·p.

bridge.name: M_pad_one_plus_side_injection_pays_A_SP
open_inputs: M_pad ≤ 1 at deployed w (toys: often true for w≥3 on F_17)
Inject CS ordered side pairs into size ≤ t·p (still open; (c0U,c0V) is p² too big)
bridge.statement: If max_z M_pad(z) ≤ 1 and, for every z, the ordered CS side pairs appearing in fiber z inject into a label set L_z with |L_z| ≤ t·p, then max N_ord ≤ t·p, hence max |A_SP| ≤ t·p (v17/v19).

noncircular.name: pair_injection_avoids_16N_circularity
proof: v19 recorded N_ord ≤ (pack−1)N as circular with Q max-fiber.
This packet's bridge uses M_pad and side-pair injection instead.
noncircular.statement: Bounding N_ord via side-pair geometry + M_pad does not route through N_ord ≤ (pack−1)N. That inequality remains true but is the circular path; the M_pad/side path is the intended non-circular alternative.

pair_nf.name: topseam_ordered_pair_normal_form
proof: v2/v16: top-seam edge ⇔ same can-core C and CS sides.
Ordered pair orients (U,V). Disjointness: free-1 CS root sets are pairwise disjoint (v2).
pair_nf.statement: Ordered top-seam pairs (S,T) in Fib_w(z) are in bijection with triples (C,U,V) where |C|=j−e, |U|=|V|=e=w+1, U∩V=∅, C∩(U∪V)=∅, S=C⊔U, T=C⊔V, and Λ_U−Λ_V is a nonzero constant (free-1 CS).

psi.name: core_augmented_pair_mark_psi
proof: Definitional mark. Injectivity: computational certificate in toy_suite for listed rows with w≥2; explicit failures at w=1.
Size n·p² vs t·p: p/t ≈ 2^{31}/2^{16} ≫ 1 so n·p² > t·p.
psi.statement:
Define ψ(C,U,V) = (min_exp(C), c0(U), c0(V)) ∈ {0..n−1}×F_p×F_p (size n·p²). This separates multi-pads that share (U,V) whenever those pads have distinct min exponents.
Toy bank: ψ is injective on all tested (p,n,j,w) with w≥2; fails at w=1.
Payment: n·p² ≫ t·p and ≫ n·p, so ψ does not by itself give an E2/E5-scale bound on N_ord. It is a uniqueness normal form, not a budget.

side_key.name: side_key_phi_and_M_pad
side_key.note: Codomain of φ is formally F_p^w × F_p × F_p (size p^{w+2}), too large for payment. The gain is structural: reduce pair injection to (a) M_pad=1 and (b) inject CS side pairs into ≤t·p labels.
proof: Free-1 monic degree e: coefficients (1, high_{e-1},...,high_1, c0) with high length e−1. Specifying high and c0 fixes the polynomial.
A monic split poly with distinct roots has unique root set.
(iii)–(v) partition pairs by side key; count.

title: Top-seam pair injection via side keys and M_pad
